x-anyshift-query-language.grammar: SELECT <*|count(*)> FROM <table> [WHERE k = v [AND ...]] [LIMIT n] [OFFSET n]
x-anyshift-query-language.valueSyntax: Values may be bare words or single- or double-quoted strings.

tables.name: resolve
tables.summary: Resolve a resource name or fragment to ranked current graph resources.

filters.name: term
filters.description: Resource name or fragment to resolve.

forms.name: Resolve resources
forms.description: Return ranked candidates for a resource name or fragment.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM resolve WHERE term = checkout LIMIT 10

tables.name: events
tables.summary: Read the infrastructure change-event timeline.

filters.name: type
filters.description: Event type or type fragment, such as oom or scaling.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Recent resource events
forms.description: Read recent events for a resource inside a time window.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM events WHERE target = checkout AND since = 2h LIMIT 20

tables.name: resources
tables.summary: Count and sample current resources of one graph resource type.

filters.name: type
filters.description: Graph resource type, such as service or deployment.

forms.name: Resource inventory
forms.description: Return the inventory for one resource type.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM resources WHERE type = deployment LIMIT 50

tables.name: connections
tables.summary: Inspect direct upstream and downstream relationships for a resource.

forms.name: Direct connections
forms.description: Return the resource and its direct graph neighbors.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM connections WHERE resource = checkout LIMIT 50

tables.name: hotspots
tables.summary: Rank noisy resources, namespaces, alert rules, or alerting workloads.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Resource hotspots
forms.description: Rank resources by recent event activity.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM hotspots WHERE by = resource AND since = 24h LIMIT 10

tables.name: incidents
tables.summary: Reconstruct a correlated incident around a target or correlation identifier.

forms.name: Incident by target
forms.description: At least one of target or id is required.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM incidents WHERE target = checkout

forms.name: Incident by correlation id
forms.description: Load one exact correlation group.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM incidents WHERE id = incident-123

tables.name: failures
tables.summary: Read recent failure-class infrastructure events.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Recent failures
forms.description: Read failures for a target, namespace, or the whole project.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM failures WHERE namespace = commerce AND since = 2h LIMIT 20

tables.name: deployments
tables.summary: Read recent workload deployments and image changes.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Recent deployments
forms.description: Read deployments for a target, namespace, or the whole project.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM deployments WHERE namespace = commerce AND since = 24h LIMIT 20

tables.name: audit
tables.summary: Read configuration, identity, and infrastructure audit events.

filters.name: type
filters.description: Audit event type or fragment, such as rbac.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: RBAC audit
forms.description: Read recent RBAC-related changes.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM audit WHERE type = rbac AND since = 24h LIMIT 20

tables.name: nodes
tables.summary: Read node lifecycle and capacity events.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Node activity
forms.description: Read recent node events.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM nodes WHERE since = 6h LIMIT 20

tables.name: deploy_impact
tables.summary: Join recent deployments to the failures that followed them.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Deployment impact
forms.description: Rank recent deployment fallout or inspect one workload.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM deploy_impact WHERE target = checkout AND since = 24h LIMIT 10

tables.name: common_cause
tables.summary: Find shared infrastructure or dependencies behind recent failures.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Shared failure causes
forms.description: Intersect recent failures by node, workload, datastore, and external dependency.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM common_cause WHERE namespace = commerce AND since = 2h LIMIT 10

tables.name: blast_radius
tables.summary: Calculate the transitive workloads, pods, and services affected by a resource.

forms.name: Resource blast radius
forms.description: Walk impact outward from one resource.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM blast_radius WHERE resource = shared-runtime-sa LIMIT 100

tables.name: spof
tables.summary: Rank highly shared ConfigMaps, service accounts, or nodes by fan-in.

filters.name: kind
filters.description: Resource kind to rank. Defaults to configmap.

forms.name: Shared service accounts
forms.description: Rank service accounts by dependent workloads and pods.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM spof WHERE kind = serviceaccount LIMIT 10

tables.name: orphans
tables.summary: Find unused or dangling Kubernetes resources.

filters.name: kind
filters.description: Resource kind to inspect. Defaults to configmap.

forms.name: Orphaned roles
forms.description: Find roles with no observed consumers.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM orphans WHERE kind = role AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

tables.name: coverage
tables.summary: Find service, monitor, or metrics coverage gaps.

forms.name: Monitoring gaps
forms.description: Find unmonitored services in one namespace.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM coverage WHERE kind = service AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

tables.name: access
tables.summary: Inspect RBAC reach or rank over-privileged service accounts.

filters.name: resource
filters.description: Subject or role name in reach mode; optional namespace scope in privileged mode.

filters.name: mode
filters.description: Access analysis mode. Defaults to reach.

values.description: Inspect what one subject can reach.

forms.name: Subject reach
forms.description: Reach mode requires resource.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM access WHERE resource = ci-deployer

forms.name: Privileged identities
forms.description: Privileged mode can optionally scope resource to a namespace.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM access WHERE mode = privileged LIMIT 10

tables.name: exposure
tables.summary: Trace public ingress exposure to or from a resource.

forms.name: Public exposure
forms.description: Trace how a resource is publicly exposed.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM exposure WHERE resource = checkout

tables.name: tenancy
tables.summary: Find workloads co-located with a resource on the same node.

forms.name: Noisy neighbors
forms.description: Inspect resources sharing a node with the target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM tenancy WHERE resource = checkout LIMIT 20

tables.name: sharedconfig
tables.summary: Find workloads coupled through shared configuration.

forms.name: Shared configuration
forms.description: Find workloads sharing configuration with the target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM sharedconfig WHERE resource = checkout LIMIT 20

tables.name: path
tables.summary: Find the shortest structural path between two resources.

forms.name: Shortest path
forms.description: Both from and to are required.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM path WHERE from = checkout AND to = checkout-postgres

tables.name: cascade
tables.summary: Trace an incident correlation group in propagation order.

forms.name: Cascade by target
forms.description: At least one of target or id is required.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM cascade WHERE target = checkout

forms.name: Cascade by correlation id
forms.description: Trace one exact correlation group.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM cascade WHERE id = incident-123

tables.name: alert_impact
tables.summary: Find monitors and SLOs affected by a resource failure.

forms.name: Alert impact
forms.description: Map an infrastructure resource to affected observability objects.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alert_impact WHERE resource = checkout

tables.name: monitor
tables.summary: Resolve a monitor to the infrastructure it observes.

forms.name: Monitor infrastructure
forms.description: Map one monitor to its service, workload, and node.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM monitor WHERE target = checkout-latency

tables.name: datastore
tables.summary: Inspect datastore dependencies or rank widely used datastores.

forms.name: Rank datastores
forms.description: Omit target to rank datastore fan-in.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM datastore LIMIT 10

forms.name: Datastore dependencies
forms.description: Inspect services connected to one datastore or datastores used by one service.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM datastore WHERE target = checkout-postgres

tables.name: flow
tables.summary: Inspect stream producers and consumers or rank busy streams.

filters.name: target
filters.description: Service, topic, queue, or stream name.

forms.name: Stream dependencies
forms.description: Inspect producers and consumers for a stream.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM flow WHERE target = checkout-events

tables.name: external_dep
tables.summary: Inspect external dependencies or rank high-fan-in external hosts.

forms.name: External dependencies
forms.description: Inspect services depending on one external host.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM external_dep WHERE target = payments.example.com

tables.name: alerts
tables.summary: List currently firing monitors, optionally scoped to a service.

forms.name: Current alerts
forms.description: List firing monitors for the project or one target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alerts WHERE target = checkout LIMIT 20

tables.name: alert_noise
tables.summary: Rank flapping or stuck monitors.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Flapping alerts
forms.description: Rank recently flapping monitors.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alert_noise WHERE kind = flapping AND since = 1d LIMIT 10

tables.name: calls
tables.summary: Inspect service callers and callees or rank call-graph fan-in.

forms.name: Service calls
forms.description: Inspect callers and callees for one service.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM calls WHERE target = checkout

tables.name: servicetree
tables.summary: Expand a service's downstream services, datastores, and external dependencies.

forms.name: Service tree
forms.description: Expand the downstream footprint of one service.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM servicetree WHERE target = checkout LIMIT 50

tables.name: alert_cause
tables.summary: Join a firing service or workload to recent Kubernetes changes.

filters.name: since
filters.description: Relative lookback such as 30m, 2h, 1d, or today.

forms.name: Alert cause
forms.description: Find recent infrastructure changes behind a firing target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alert_cause WHERE target = checkout AND since = 2h LIMIT 20

tables.name: slo
tables.summary: Inspect one SLO or rank breaching and at-risk SLOs.

forms.name: SLO health
forms.description: Inspect one SLO by name.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM slo WHERE target = 'checkout availability'

tables.name: alertrules
tables.summary: Inspect Grafana and VictoriaMetrics alert-rule coverage and inventory.

filters.name: namespace
filters.description: Namespace scope for coverage or inventory.

filters.name: target
filters.description: Service or workload name. Required when subject is target.

forms.name: Alert-rule coverage
forms.description: Find services or workloads without alert rules.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alertrules WHERE subject = coverage AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

forms.name: Rules for a target
forms.description: Target subject requires target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM alertrules WHERE subject = target AND target = checkout

tables.name: gitops
tables.summary: Inspect GitOps drift, unmanaged workloads, or resource ownership.

values.description: Find the application and repository owning one resource.

filters.name: namespace
filters.description: Namespace scope for drift or unmanaged views.

filters.name: resource
filters.description: Workload name. Required when subject is owner.

forms.name: GitOps drift
forms.description: List drifted applications, optionally scoped to a namespace.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM gitops WHERE subject = drift AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

forms.name: Resource owner
forms.description: Owner subject requires resource.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM gitops WHERE subject = owner AND resource = checkout

tables.name: image
tables.summary: Inspect image usage, workload containers, or container hygiene gaps.

filters.name: workload
filters.description: Workload whose container resources should be inspected.

filters.name: namespace
filters.description: Namespace scope for a hygiene scan.

forms.name: Image usage
forms.description: Inspect who runs an image or what image a target runs.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM image WHERE target = checkout

forms.name: Container hygiene
forms.description: Kind selects a hygiene scan and namespace optionally scopes it.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM image WHERE kind = nomemlimit AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

tables.name: netpol
tables.summary: Inspect NetworkPolicy coverage, policies, or east-west reach.

filters.name: mode
filters.description: Network policy view. Defaults to uncovered.

values.description: Inspect policies for a target or namespace.
Model workloads that could reach a target.

filters.name: namespace
filters.description: Namespace scope for uncovered or policy views.

filters.name: target
filters.description: Workload or policy name. Required for segmentation.

forms.name: NetworkPolicy gaps
forms.description: Find default-allow namespaces.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM netpol WHERE mode = uncovered LIMIT 20

forms.name: East-west reach
forms.description: Segmentation mode requires target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM netpol WHERE mode = segmentation AND target = checkout

tables.name: priority
tables.summary: Inspect scheduling priority gaps, the class ladder, or one target's priority.

values.description: Find workloads without a priority class.
List the observed priority class ladder.

filters.name: target
filters.description: Workload or pod name whose priority should be inspected.

forms.name: Missing priority classes
forms.description: Find workloads without a priority class.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM priority WHERE kind = nopriority AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

forms.name: Target priority
forms.description: Inspect the priority class for one workload or pod.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM priority WHERE target = checkout

tables.name: storage
tables.summary: Inspect workload storage and find orphaned or unclaimed volumes.

values.description: Trace a workload's PVC, PV, and StorageClass chain.
Group or filter storage by StorageClass.

filters.name: workload
filters.description: Workload or pod name. Required in footprint mode.

filters.name: resource
filters.description: Alias for workload in footprint mode.

filters.name: class
filters.description: StorageClass filter for orphanpv or byclass mode.

forms.name: Workload storage
forms.description: Footprint mode requires workload or resource.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM storage WHERE workload = checkout

forms.name: Unclaimed PVCs
forms.description: Find unclaimed claims, optionally scoped to a namespace.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM storage WHERE mode = unclaimedpvc AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

tables.name: pdb
tables.summary: Find workloads without PodDisruptionBudgets or inspect one workload or PDB.

forms.name: PDB coverage gaps
forms.description: Omit filters to list workloads without PDB protection.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM pdb LIMIT 20

forms.name: Target PDB coverage
forms.description: Inspect one workload or PDB by target, workload, or pdb.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM pdb WHERE workload = checkout

tables.name: scaling
tables.summary: Find workloads without HPAs, list autoscaled workloads, or inspect one target.

filters.name: namespace
filters.description: Namespace scope for nohpa or autoscaled views.

filters.name: target
filters.description: Workload or HPA name. Required in target mode.

forms.name: Autoscaling gaps
forms.description: Find workloads without HPAs.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM scaling WHERE mode = nohpa AND namespace = commerce LIMIT 20

forms.name: Target autoscaling
forms.description: Target mode requires target.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM scaling WHERE target = checkout

tables.name: topology
tables.summary: Build a typed service topology at a selected level.

values.description: Runtime containers, workloads, datastores, queues, and external dependencies.
Ordered call flow suitable for a sequence diagram.

forms.name: Service topology
forms.description: Service is required; level selects the topology depth.
forms.example: SELECT * FROM topology WHERE service = checkout AND level = context

info.title: Anyshift Graph API
info.description: Read-only API for querying Anyshift infrastructure graphs by project.

tags.name: Graph
tags.description: Ask natural-language questions or run deterministic graph queries.

ProjectId.name: projectId
ProjectId.description: Anyshift project id whose graph should be queried.

sql.description: SELECT <*|count(*)> FROM <table> [WHERE k = v [AND ...]] [LIMIT n] [OFFSET n]

post.summary: Run a query-language query (deterministic, no LLM).
Run a deterministic graph query for an Anyshift project

500.description: Project events graph unconfigured or internal error

post.summary: Ask a natural-language graph question for an Anyshift project

500.description: Project events graph unconfigured or internal error
